Title: Create NEW ticket happy path
Preconditions: User logged in and has access to create ticket
Test Data: ticketType=NEW, priority=HIGH, managerEmail valid, description >=10 chars, address valid
Steps: Open Create Ticket form; fill mandatory fields; click Submit
Expected Result: Ticket is created successfully and shown in requester list/dashboard with OPEN status

Title: Create REPAIR ticket happy path
Preconditions: User has at least one asset in My Assets
Test Data: ticketType=REPAIR, assetId valid, priority=MEDIUM, managerEmail valid, description valid
Steps: Open Create Ticket form; select REPAIR; select asset; submit
Expected Result: Ticket is created with OPEN status and linked to selected asset

Title: Required field validation on create
Test Data: ticketType missing OR priority missing OR managerEmail missing OR description missing
Steps: Attempt submit with one required field empty
Expected Result: Inline validation prevents submit and shows correct message

Title: Conditional field behavior by ticket type
Steps: Select NEW then REPAIR then NEW again
Expected Result: Asset field shown only for REPAIR and address shown only for NEW

Title: Description length validation
Expected Result: Validation errors shown for min and max length boundaries

Title: Manager email format validation
Expected Result: Submit blocked with valid email message

Title: Manager approval email trigger after create
Expected Result: Manager receives approval email with ticket number and requester details

Title: No duplicate ticket on double click
Steps: Click submit repeatedly while request pending
Expected Result: Only one ticket record is created and one ticket number returned

Title: Unauthorized create request handling
Expected Result: User gets session-expired flow and ticket is not created

Title: Server error handling on create
Expected Result: User sees error feedback and remains on form with no duplicate state

Title: Ticket appears in manager pending approval queue
Expected Result: Ticket is visible with correct metadata and actionable controls

Title: Manager approves ticket
Preconditions: Ticket is OPEN and pending manager action
Test Data: Approval comment optional/required as per rule
Expected Result: Ticker status transitions to APPROVED (or approved equivalent) and audit entry is created

Title: Manager rejects ticket
Preconditions: Ticket is OPEN and pending manager action
Steps: Manager clicks Reject and submits reason
Expected Result: Ticket transitions to REJECTED/CLOSED as per configured workflow and requester can view reason

Title: Only manager can approve or reject
Expected Result: Action is hidden or denied by API with no status change

Title: Approval idempotency (double approve)
Expected Result: Second attempt does not create duplicate transitions or notifications

Title: Post-approval assignment to IT coordinator
Preconditions: Ticket approved and assignment workflow enabled
Steps: Assign ticket to IT coordinator/ops lead/IT lead
Expected Result: Status moves to ASSIGNED and assignee is visible in ticket details

Title: Escalation from assigned state
Expected Result: Ticket moves to ESCALATED path with proper owner/audit trail

Title: Move assigned ticket to in progress
Steps: Assigned user starts work and updates status
Expected Result: Ticket transitions to IN_PROGRESS and timeline is recorded

Title: Resolve ticket after work completion
Expected Result: Ticket transitions to RESOLVED and appears in resolved filter/card

Title: Allocation creation allowed only after approval
Preconditions: Ticket not approved and then approved
Steps: Try create allocation before and after approval
Expected Result: Before approval blocked; after approval allowed

Title: Role-based allocation permission
Expected Result: Allowed roles succeed; other roles denied

Title: Create allocation happy path from approved ticket
Test Data: asset set, campus, assignee, allocation metadata
Expected Result: Allocation created with ALLOCATED status and ticket-allocation linkage saved

Title: Prevent duplicate allocation for same ticket
Preconditions: One active allocation already exists for ticket
Expected Result: Second active allocation is blocked or flagged per business rule

Title: Allocation completion transition
Expected Result: Allocation transitions to ALLOCATION_COMPLETED and ticket can proceed to downstream states

Title: Allocation visibility in tracking dashboards
Expected Result: Allocation status and linked ticket info are visible and consistent

Title: Create consignment from allocation details
Test Data: Allocation id with one or more assets
Expected Result: Consignment created in DRAFT/PACKED(Ready to dispatch) as per configured state

Title: Multiple consignments from same allocation when allowed
Steps: Create two consignments with asset split
Expected Result: Each consignment maintains unique tracking state and no asset overlap

Title: Dispatch consignment happy path
Expected Result: Consignment transitions to DISPATCHED and dispatch metadata saved

Title: Dispatch validation for missing tracking info
Expected Result: Validation blocks dispatch and status remains unchanged

Title: Track action availability by status
Preconditions: Consignment in draft and dispatched states
Expected Result: Track button visible only when dispatched and tracking id valid

Title: Delivered transition happy path
Expected Result: Status transitions to DELIVERED and delivery timestamp is recorded

Title: Non-delivery handling and ticket closure control
Steps: Mark undelivered scenario and perform admin action
Expected Result: Ticket is not auto-closed; explicit admin path controls closure/escalation per rule

Title: End-to-end NEW flow status chain
Steps: Run full flow: Raised->Open->Approve->Assign->In Progress->Resolved + Allocation->Allocated->Completed + Consignment->Packed->Dispatch->Delivered
Expected Result: All statuses update in correct sequence with valid links and audit records

Title: Cross-module data integrity on IDs and references
Preconditions: At least one full lifecycle record exists
Test Data: Ticket id allocation id consignment id
Steps: Verify linkage across details/list views and APIs
Expected Result: Same references are preserved across Ticket Allocation Consignment modules

Title: Dashboard counters and filters reflect transitions
Steps: Apply filters and compare counts to API data
Expected Result: Counters and filtered lists remain consistent after each transition

Title: Audit trail completeness for critical transitions
Steps: Perform approval assignment allocation dispatch delivery actions
Expected Result: Audit contains actor role timestamp old->new status and linked entity id

Title: Notification consistency across stages
Test Data: One ticket through approval and dispatch
Expected Result: Configured users receive expected notifications exactly once

Title: Concurrency control on status updates
Preconditions: Two users open same ticket/consignment simultaneously
Steps: Submit conflicting status changes at same time
Expected Result: System prevents invalid final state and returns deterministic conflict handling

Title: SLA and overdue behavior for unresolved tickets
Test Data: Ticket kept in open/in progress beyond SLA
Steps: Wait or backdate and run SLA job/view
Expected Result: Tickets are marked overdue without corrupting allocation/consignment links

Title: Business-rule clarification checks from open questions
Test Data: auto-close timeout, rejection closure, assignment routing logic
Steps: Run targeted checks for each agreed rule
Expected Result: Behavior matches finalized rulebook and documented acceptance criteria
